english: relations between Tibet and China Glr.; pyág-gi tsúl-du (phyag gi tshul du) in a way as if he were saluting Mil.; gus-gús-kyi tsúl(-du) byéd-pa (gus gus kyi tshul du byed pa) to make a semblance of veneration, to make gestures of reverence Mil.; mi mǩyén-pai tsúl-du byás-te (mi mkhyen pa'i tshul du byas te) pretending not to know Mil.; (cf. tsúl-čos-pa (tshul chos pa) v. čos-pa (chos pa)); dge-slóṅ-gi tsúl-du (dge slong gi tshul du) in the guise of a monk Tar.; maí tsul ₒdzín-pa (ma'i tshul 'dzin pa) to assume the mother's form, figure Tar.; glaṅ-čéṅ-gyi tsúl-du (glang chen gyi tshul du), (Buddha came down) in the shape of, or as, an elephant Glr.; dád-pai tsúl-gyis (dad pa'i tshul gyis) in the way of faith, with a believing mind Pth.; mi-rtág tsúl-du yda (mi rtag tshul du yda) it exists in the way of transientness, it is of a transitory nature Mil.; mdzód-pa bču-yñyís-kyi tsúl-gyis (mdzod pa bcu gnyis kyi tshul gyis) in the manner, in the order, of the twelve deeds Glr.; šas čé-bai tsúl-gyis (shas che ba'i tshul gyis) for the most part, Tar. 50, 15; way of acting, conduct, deportment, course of life, sṅá-mai tsul (snga ma'i tshul) your former conduct Mil; dé-lta-buí dgé-baí tsul de tós-nas (de lta bu'i dge ba'i tshul de thos nas) hearing such an example of virtue related. — 2. emphat.: the right way, good manners, order, rule: tsul (daṅ) mtún(-pa) (tshul dang mthun pa) orderly, regular, sensible, reasonable, brgyá-la tsul-mtún re tsam ₒbyúṅ-na (brgya la tshul mthun re tsam 'byung na) Mil. if but once in a hundred cases something sensible is uttered; tsul-ldán (tshul ldan), tsúl-čan (tshul can) regular, methodical Cs.; also just, conformable to duty, tsúl-bžin-pa (tshul bzhin pa) adv. tsul-bžin-du (tshul bzhin du) id.; tsul-méd (tshul med), tsul-bžin-mín (tshul bzhin min) irregular, unjust Cs.; srid-žui tsul spyód-čiṅ (srid zhu'i tshul spyod cing) fulfilling a child's duty; tsúl-las ñyams (tshul las nyams) growing remiss in one's duty, neglecting, breaking one's duty; esp. tsúl-krims (tshul khrims) religious or moral duty, moral law; monastic vows, tsúl-krims-čan (tshul khrims can) 1. being bound by such Sch.; 2. observing such Cs.; tsúl-krims srúṅ-ba (tshul khrims srung ba) to keep them, ₒjíg-pa ('jig pa), ñyáms-pa (nyams pa) to break them; tsul-krims (tshul khrims), as a personal name, is much in favour. — 3. species, kind, nád-tsul (nad tshul) species or kind of disease, zás-tsul (zas tshul) species of food S.g. (not frq.). — 4. joined to the root of a verb: yóṅ tsul (yong tshul), when, or as, he came, W.

I. num. figure: 108. II. sbst. 1. time, in a gen. sense, = dus (dus) B.; yód(-pai) tse(-na) (yod pa'i tshe na), when it is, when it was; gáṅ(-gi) tse(-na) (gang gi tshe na), de(i) tse(-na) (de'i tshe na) at which time, at that time, then, frq. tse-ré (tshe re) all the time (?), nyin-tse-ré (nyin tshe re) the whole day, tsan-tse-ré (mtshan tshe re) the whole night W. — 2. time of life, *tse-ghaṅ-tsón-čug* (tshe gang btson bcug) imprisonment for life C.; tse gčíg-gi drós-pai gos (tshe gcig gi dros pa'i gos) v. drós-pa (dros pa); life, tse ₒdí (tshe 'di) this, the present, life, tse-pyí(-ma) (tshe phyi ma) a future period of life (also merely: ₒdí pyí ('di phyi), without tse (tshe)); tse sṅá-ma (tshe snga ma) an earlier period of existence, relative to the transmigration of souls, yet tse ₒdí (tshe 'di) and pyí (phyi) may also be used in a Christian sense; tse ríṅ-ba (tshe ring ba) long life, tse túṅ-ba (tshe thung ba) short life; tse-ríṅ (tshe ring) is also a very common name both of men and women; rgyál-ba daṅ tse-ríṅ-bar šóg-čig (rgyal ba dang tshe ring bar shog cig) happiness and long life (to the king)! Dzl.; tse(-daṅ)-ldán(-pa) (tshe dang ldan pa), आयुष्मन्, title or epithet of Bodhisattwas; tse-dpag-méd (tshe dpag med) name of Buddha; *tse píd-če* (tshe phid ce) W. to earn a livelihood; tse ǩyér-žin šór-ba (tshe khyer zhin shor ba) to come off with one's life, to have a narrow escape; tse tár-du ₒjúg-pa (tshe thar du 'jug pa) v. tár-ba (thar ba); tse(-las) ₒdás(-pa) (tshe las 'das pa) having died Dzl. — 3. Bal. sex, *pó-tse* (pho tshe), *mó-tse* (mo tshe), male, female sex. Comp. tse-skabs (tshe skabs) v. skabs (skabs). — tse-čú (tshe chu) water of life Glr. — tse-yñyís-pa (tshe gnyis pa) of an amphibious nature Cs. — tse-ltógs (tshe ltogs) a poor, starving vagrant, beggar W. — tse-mdáṅs (tshe mdangs) Lt. = byad-mdáṅs (byad mdangs) healthy appearance, a fine, fresh complexion. — tse-tsád (tshe tshad) duration of life. — tse-mdzad (tshe mdzad), Wilk. 457, an attribute of the gods, resembling a small plate with fruit. — tse-rábs (tshe rabs) period of existence, duration of a re-birth, a great many of which acc. to Buddhist doctrine every man has to pass through Dzl.; tse-rábs-kyi blá-ma (tshe rabs kyi bla ma) Mil. a man that is always re-born as a Lama.
tse-pád (tshe pad) Ephedra saxatilis, a little alpine shrub with red berries, which are said to be roasted and pulverized, to give greater pungency to snuff.
tse-ré (tshe re) 1. v. tse (tshe). — 2. v. tsér-ka (tsher ka).

tseg (tsheg) W. *tsag* (tshag) 1. point, dot, also nag-tség (nag tsheg). — 2. more particularly the point separating syllables, bar-tség (bar tsheg), id.; pyi-tség (phyi tsheg) likewise, in as far as it follows a letter Gram.; tseg-bár (tsheg bar) that which stands between two points or tsegs (tshegs), a syllable.
